title: Partial Truth
story: [Meridian-7 Log #009] Timestamps have appeared in the data packets, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD. Does rEx use a calendar similar to humans? Or... are these timestamps formatted so you can understand them? Not all data that looks like dates is valid — you must filter out corrupted entries.
promptChallenge: Write a prompt to have AI generate a regex that matches dates in YYYY-MM-DD format (month 01-12, day 01-31)
hints: [Hint] You need character ranges to restrict valid digit values
promptHints: Tell the AI the exact date format and valid ranges
Clarify the upper and lower bounds for month and day
Have the AI note boundary values: 00 is not a valid month

feedback.onPass: [Pattern Accepted] Timestamps recognized. rEx's signal has a temporal dimension.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Date validation failed. Your constraints need to be more precise.
feedback.onPerfect: [Decryption Success] Timeline perfectly aligned. Every date is a coordinate.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual time calibration. Your regex is precise to the day.
